channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Pi Network Team Reveals New Update Deadline Amid PI Price Turmoil
item.description: The native token's rebound yesterday turned out to be a classic dead-cat bounce....
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: A bitcoin wallet dormant since the 2017 peak just moved $383 million
item.description: The coins went to a fresh address rather than an exchange, so nothing has been sold yet....
item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.description: Nearly a dozen vulnerable and now revoked UEFI shim bootloaders remained trusted for years, giving attackers a path to bypass Secure Boot....

item.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
item.description: Email attacks overtook exploits as the top ransomware root cause last year. Multifactor authentication (MFA) was deployed in 97% of credential-based attacks but failed to prevent compromise....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.description: Five malicious versions of AsyncAPI packages were published to the Node Package Manager (npm) in a supply-chain attack that delivered a remote access trojan with info-stealing capabilities. [...]...

item.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
item.description: Progress Software has confirmed that a high-severity zero-day vulnerability is behind the emergency shutdown of ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers last week and has released security updates to patch ...

item.title: Study of 85 Crypto Wallet Extensions Finds Address Leaks and Cross-Site Tracking Risks
item.description:
Researchers at KU Leuven tested 85 of the most popular crypto wallets that run as browser extensions and found that the wallets themselves leak enough to link and track the people using them.
The way...
